results.title: Examining AI Agent Traffic: Powering the Shift to Agentic Commerce
results.content:
The merchants who will thrive in agentic commerce aren’t just tracking these trends—they’re building the infrastructure to see agent behavior in real-time and govern it dynamically. That means knowing which agents are visiting your site, understanding their intent across the customer journey, and having granular controls over what they’re allowed to do.

That’s what AgenticTrust delivers.

### See. Govern. Grow.

See consumer agents in action. Get session-level visibility into which AI agents are browsing your catalog, attempting logins, or reaching checkout. Understand their behavior patterns, evaluate trustworthiness, and identify whether activity was user-prompted or autonomous. [...] Stacked bar chart titled “Monthly AI Agent Traffic” showing the growth of agent-driven web requests from July to September 2025. ChatGPT Agent dominates in July and August, while Perplexity’s Comet Browser overtakes in September, driving roughly half of total agentic traffic. Other minor sources—such as Skyvern, Browseruse, Copilot Actions, and Genspark—remain small throughout.

AI agent traffic more than tripled from July to September 2025, driven primarily by the commercial launch of ChatGPT Agent and Perplexity Comet.

#### Your Guide to Safely Adopting Agentic Commerce

See how AI agents are changing discovery and purchase, explore the emerging trust frameworks, and learn what readiness looks like for the agent-driven economy.

Read the Guide [...] In early 2025, this traffic was primarily open-source agents used for testing or as developer tools, but as the year went on, large commercially released agents came to dominate. Agentic traffic grew sharply from Q2 to Q3, largely due to the July releases of ChatGPT Agent and Perplexity Comet,  and has continued to grow quickly. Total AI agent traffic increased by 131.15% month over month from August to September.

results.title: AI Discoverability on the Agentic Web: Can AI Find and Recommend Your Business? | Rukmini Iyer posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content:
There was a time when the only thing standing between a brand and its customer was a search bar. Today, there's an AI agent — and it's already making choices for us.
Agentic traffic has grown exponentially over the past year.
72% of customers now expect shopping experiences guided by agents that anticipate their needs, walk them through decisions, and complete transactions frictionlessly.
Traditional SEO is no longer enough. Brands need to be found and chosen — by humans and agents alike.
And while this transformation reshapes the market outside, something equally significant is building within organisations: the Frontier Firm — where people and agents work side by side, turning ambition, ideas and intelligence into entirely new business models. [...] Paste that prompt into Claude, GPT, or Gemini, fill in your details, iterate until the plan fits your team.
Then plug the agent specs into Google’s setup to get real-time alerts.
Want a daily shortcut that tells you exactly what to ask AI to grow your business?
Follow me to get daily shortcuts! [...] Google released a new AI visibility report that only shows impressions. The internet is mad because it doesn’t have clicks.
But most marketers are making a big mistake: equating Google search impressions (virtually useless) with AI impressions (actually pretty impactful).
The data shows that when people see your brand in LLMs, they convert better. A lot better.
And in AI, you’re typically cited alongside up to 4 other brands, not the 5 ads and 10 search results in Google.
Yes, without click data, tracking the impact is harder. But it only takes 2 minutes of configuration in GA4 or Hubspot to see clicks from LLMs anyway. But having no source-of-truth data on AI search was an unsolved problem, so I’m glad this report is here.
Link to the AI Visibility Report in the comments

results.title: Allvue Agentic AI Platform | Allvue Systems
results.content:
## Agentic AI Purpose-Built for the Alternatives Market

Allvue’s Agentic AI Platform is purpose-built for the alternatives market, combining deep domain expertise with generative AI to help investment teams move faster, work smarter, and scale efficiently. It delivers intelligent support within Allvue’s platform, giving users real-time guidance and contextual answers. Designed to evolve, the platform will introduce a growing suite of agentic capabilities that surface investment insights, automate routine steps, and enhance decision-making across front to back-office operations.

icon-charcoal-datawarehouse

### Deliver Contextual Intelligence [...] ## What is Allvue’s Agentic AI Platform?

Allvue’s Agentic AI Platform is an AI infrastructure layer purpose-built for the alternative investment industry, combining generative AI with deep domain expertise to embed intelligence directly into fund accounting, credit front-office, compliance, and trade order management workflows. The platform’s first deployed capability is Andi, an AI assistant available via Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge browser extensions. Document IQ, an AI-powered data extraction solution for credit financial spreading, is the platform’s second capability. The platform is designed to expand into a growing suite of AI agents for autonomous workflow execution, predictive analytics, and cross-platform support across Allvue’s 21,000+ fund ecosystem. [...] As the first agentic AI platform purpose-built for the alternatives market, Allvue delivers targeted intelligence that reflects the real needs of investment professionals. Designed to support daily workflows and scale with evolving operations, the platform embeds knowledge, context, and secure infrastructure, so AI works for your strategy, not around it.

Purpose-built AI for private equity, credit, and hybrid strategies aligned to alternative investment workflows.

Evolving from insights to intelligent action, executing tasks, triggering workflows, and driving better decisions.

Adapts to your firm’s structure, delivering role-specific answers and faster, smarter support.

Built for trust with SOC 2 compliance, security, and seamless integration across investment operations.

results.title: Agentic AI Is Redefining Marketing Growth | BCG
results.content:
## Market and Customer Intelligence: From Insight to Enterprise Value Creation

Theodore Levitt famously observed that customers don’t want a quarter-inch drill; they want a quarter-inch hole. Despite that insight, the data most marketers use has remained stubbornly stuck at the drill level, tracking categories and clicks rather than the outcomes customers are trying to achieve. [...] Yet many companies have a customer-shaped hole at the center of their strategy, not because of a lack of data but because they lack the organizational wiring to translate consumer intelligence into decisions. BCG research shows a familiar set of barriers: siloed data and teams, disconnected incentives, technology gaps, and intelligence that reaches decision makers too late to matter.

With agentic AI data, marketing can become the system through which customer intent directly informs enterprise decision-making. For perhaps the first time, the customer can have a true seat at the table through continuously updated signals that shape what the organization builds, delivers, and prioritizes.

For CMOs, these are the implications: [...] What’s new isn’t simply more data. It’s a fundamentally different type of customer intelligence that reveals both cross-category competitive dynamics and the contextual factors driving customer choices. When marketers can see the greater context around customer intent and decision making, they can identify where existing offerings fail to meet customer needs and where new value can be created. These insights inform not only positioning and go-to-market strategy but also product development, bundling, partnerships, and new business models. This new intelligence can directly influence what the company builds, not just how it markets what it already sells.
